news.title: Mumbai: EOW closes ₹67.5 crore redevelopment cheating case against Kamala Mills owner
news.para: Mumbai police's economic offences wing has closed a Rs 67.50 crore cheating case against Kamala Mills owner Ramesh Govani. The EOW filed a 'B' summary report, citing insufficient evidence to substantiate the complainant's allegations. Investigations revealed a mutual cancellation of the agreement between the parties.

news.title: TCS scales up Chennai presence to 7.79 lakh sq ft with fresh OMR lease
news.para: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has expanded its Chennai office space by 1.47 lakh sq ft at Ozone Techno Park, increasing its total footprint to approximately 7.79 lakh sq ft. This strategic move reinforces Chennai's importance as a key delivery hub for TCS, aligning with its hybrid work strategies and talent availability.

news.title: WeWork India to add over 4,100 desks in Bengaluru with ₹60 crore investment
news.para: WeWork India is set to broaden its footprint in Bengaluru with two fresh lease agreements, bringing in more than 4,100 additional workspaces. These innovative office hubs are projected to be up and running by June 2026, with an investment of around ₹60 crore. This strategic expansion underscores the soaring appetite for flexible work environments in the vibrant city.

news.title: Legal hurdles remain for buyers of Jaypee Sports City amidst Adani Enterprises' takeover of JAL
news.para: Adani Enterprises' Rs 14,535-crore resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL) has been approved by the NCLT. However, the fate of the stalled Jaypee Sports City project and thousands of homebuyers rests on a Supreme Court battle over land allotment cancellation due to unpaid dues.

news.title: Gurugram authorities to take strict action against erring builders over delay in audit work
news.para: The Gurugram administration is setting firm guidelines for builders, imposing penalties on those who procrastinate on vital repair tasks. While structural assessments in numerous societies are concluded, the much-needed enhancements are lagging. Builders now have a strict 15-day deadline to kickstart repairs. In case of non-compliance, resident welfare associations are empowered to initiate repairs, provided they adhere to safety standards.

news.title: Hyderabad: CCS registers case against two builders for pre-launch investment fraud
news.para: Central Crime Station sleuths have registered a case against directors of Bharathi Builders for allegedly duping a city-based businessman and his brother of ₹87 lakh. The directors are accused of promising double the principal investment within 36 months on a pre-launch gated community project.

news.title: Nashik-based builder arrested for EWS quota violation across 49 projects
news.para: A developer has been arrested in Nashik for allegedly cheating 197 landlords, builders, and developers by bypassing the mandatory 20% EWS reservation in residential projects. Accused of using forged documents and splitting land parcels to evade the quota, the FIR was registered following a committee's findings on state housing policies.

news.title: Haryana RERA orders ₹21 lakh payment to buyer who booked flat in 2008, didn't get it
news.para: A Gurugram developer has been ordered by Rera to pay over Rs 21 lakh to a homebuyer for delayed possession. The compensation includes Rs 18.9 lakh for missed property appreciation, Rs 2 lakh for mental harassment, and Rs 50,000 for litigation costs. This ruling emphasizes that homebuyers can claim additional compensation beyond refunds for delays.

news.title: MLA alleges ₹2,000 crore housing scam in Mumbai, government orders audit
news.para: The Maharashtra government has launched a vigilance audit into a reported Rs 2,000 crore housing scam in Mumbai. Prompted by concerns from a BJP MLA about developers exploiting housing scheme loopholes, this thorough investigation aims to uncover any irregularities within 30 days.

news.title: ED restores ₹650-crore assets to over 2,300 home buyers of SRS realty projects
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has returned immovable assets valued at Rs 650 crore to more than 2,300 home buyers. This action stems from a money laundering investigation into the SRS Group. The agency utilized a special provision under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act. Promoters of the SRS Group allegedly lured investors and home buyers with promises of high returns.

news.title: Signature Global sees ₹35,000 crore free cash flow from pipeline, bets on commercial real estate with RMZ
news.para: Signature Global anticipates generating ₹35,000 crore in free cash flow. The company is entering commercial real estate with a partnership with RMZ. This move aims to build a platform for large office developments in Gurugram. Signature Global also plans launches worth ₹40,000-50,000 crore over the next three years. The focus is shifting towards execution and asset creation.

news.title: UK's Vistry Group appoints Rob Woodward as chairman
news.para: British homebuilder Vistry Group has a new non-executive chair. Rob Woodward takes over on May 13. He previously led STV Group. The company is still searching for a new chief executive. Outgoing chair and CEO Greg Fitzgerald retires in May. Vistry Group faces a slow housing market. The company warned of lower margins for fiscal 2026.

news.title: US Bancorp in talks to lease 6.5 lakh sq ft in Chennai for GCC
news.para: US Bancorp is set to establish its first global capability centre in India, leasing over 650,000 sq ft in Chennai. The banking giant is also exploring additional space in Bengaluru and Hyderabad, signaling a significant expansion of its offshore operations and a strategic move to insource technology and operational capabilities.

news.title: Sambhajinagar residents demand waiver in property tax similar to Mumbai
news.para: Citizen groups in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar are demanding a property tax waiver for houses up to 1,000 square feet, mirroring Mumbai's relief. They argue for equal treatment, regardless of location, while the municipal corporation faces financial constraints compared to Mumbai's vast revenue.

news.title: Hubballi-Dharwad civic bosy looks to collect ₹333 crore property tax in FY27
news.para: Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation presented a Rs 7.7 crore surplus budget for 2026-27, projecting Rs 1,603.2 crore in revenue and Rs 1,594.4 crore in expenditure. Key proposals include an 80% increase in property tax collection and significant allocations for capital expenditure and ward development.

news.title: Noida airport effect: 54 new industries, 700 lease deeds executed in a year
news.para: Industrial activity around the upcoming Noida International Airport has surged, with a significant increase in lease deeds executed and units taking physical possession of plots. This growth, reflecting rising investor interest, is further evidenced by a sharp rise in sanctioned building plans and units under construction, signaling the region's transformation into a major industrial hub.

news.title: Prayagraj Junction to emerge as a major commercial hub; seven floors to be leased out
news.para: Prayagraj Junction's redevelopment project, costing Rs 960 crore, is set to transform it into a commercial hub. Railway authorities are leasing seven floors of the new nine-storey building to private investors for 45 years to develop malls, hotels, and offices. This initiative aims to provide airport-like facilities and secure stable revenue for the railways.

news.title: About 1.6 lakh notices issued to property tax defaulters by civic bodies in Telangana
news.para: Civic authorities in Hyderabad's Core Urban Region (CURE) are intensifying efforts to recover pending property taxes, issuing over 1.64 lakh notices to defaulters. With 40% of property owners yet to pay, a significant Rs 4,201 crore remains outstanding against a total demand of Rs 6,387 crore. A One-Time Settlement Scheme offering a 90% interest rebate is available until March 31.

news.title: Haryana eyes Ambala’s expansion with 6,600-acre land buyback
news.para: Haryana government is acquiring over 6,600 acres for Ambala's urban expansion through a digital, consent-based process. Farmers in seven villages can voluntarily sell land via the e-Bhoomi portal, setting their own prices. This initiative aims to build new residential and commercial hubs with modern infrastructure, avoiding traditional acquisition disputes.
